item.title: San Diego Islamic Center victims ID'd, security guard remembered as hero
item.description: A security guard who was among the victims killed in a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego is being hailed for his heroism and bravery during the attack.

item.title: What to know about today's primaries in Georgia, Kentucky and more
item.description: Polls are beginning to close Tuesday for primaries in several states, including the most expensive House primary in history.

item.title: UAE claims Iran-backed Shiite militias in Iraq attacked nuclear power plant
item.description: The United Arab Emirates on Tuesday alleged that Iran-backed Shiite militias in Iraq attacked one of its nuclear power plants. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean joins with more.

item.title: Examining redistricting's net effect on 2026 midterms
item.description: The redistricting battle between Republicans and Democrats across the U.S. will shape the November midterms. Kyle Kondik, CBS News contributor and managing editor of Sabato's Crystal Ball at the University of Virginia Center for Politics, joins to discuss.

item.title: Primaries to watch in Kentucky, Pennsylvania
item.description: Kentucky and Pennsylvania are among the states holding primary contests on Tuesday night. CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto joins with analysis.

item.title: Georgia holding closely-watched primaries for Senate and governor
item.description: Georgia voters are at the polls on Tuesday for primary day, casting ballots in two primary races with national attention: Senate and governor. CBS News correspondent Skyler Henry reports from Atlanta.

item.title: Senate advances resolution to limit Trump's Iran war powers for first time
item.description: Four Republicans voted with nearly all Democrats to advance the Iran war powers measure, with GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy joining the Republican defectors for the first time.

item.title: Army soldier says wife was arrested by ICE despite doing the "right thing"
item.description: U.S. Army Sergeant First Class Jose Serrano tells CBS News that ICE arrested his wife, Deisy Rivera Ortega, even though they were following the rules.

item.title: Voters want more competitive congressional districts, CBS News poll finds
item.description: But most GOP voters want candidates who'd back most or everything Trump wants.

item.title: U.S. intel assessment says military identified at least 10 mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: CBS News was unable to determine what type of mines were in involved in this latest assessment.

item.title: Mangione supporters should not have been given press passes, mayor says
item.description: Luigi Mangione supporters have loudly made their feelings known outside every court appearance, but several are now in court with official press passes.

item.title: Trump says he was "an hour away" from striking Iran
item.description: President Trump says he scrapped a planned attack on Iran at the request of Gulf allies as "serious negotiations" on a peace deal are underway.

item.title: Rep. Thomas Massie ahead of primary day: "I've never even said an unkind word about President Trump"
item.description: Republican Rep. Thomas Massie of Kentucky is looking to defeat Trump-backed challenger Ed Gallrein in his GOP primary on Tuesday night. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe spoke with Massie on Monday about the race.

item.title: Trump showdown with Massie comes to a head in Kentucky primary
item.description: For weeks, President Trump has been on a crusade to impose party discipline by devoting his energy and millions upon millions in GOP dollars to defeating wayward Republicans. The biggest contest of Mr. Trump's purity passion play comes on Tuesday night: Rep. Thomas Massie's primary race against Ed Gallrein in Kentucky. CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett has more.

item.title: DOJ's settlement with Trump bans IRS from taking action over his old tax returns
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service is permanently barred from pursuing claims against President Trump or his company based on prior tax returns, part of a controversial settlement deal between the Justice Department and Mr. Trump.

item.title: Privacy concerns grow after AI chatbots give out people's real phone numbers
item.description: Privacy concerns about artificial intelligence are growing after people discovered that chatbots are sharing their real phone numbers. Eileen Guo, investigative tech reporter for MIT Technology Review, joins to discuss.

item.title: AI sparks backlash from new graduates. How deep does the disapproval go?
item.description: Many Americans are signaling disapproval of the technology amid fears that it will eclipse already competitive entry-level jobs.

item.title: Islamic Center of San Diego imam on victims of shooting: "They sacrificed their lives"
item.description: Imam Taha Hassan of the Islamic Center of San Diego tells CBS News about the beloved security guard and members of his mosque who died "sacrificed their lives for the entire community" during Monday's shooting.

item.title: NTSB questions Boeing, other aviation officials over deadly UPS plane crash in Kentucky
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board on Tuesday questioned UPS, Boeing and other aviation officials about maintenance and possible safety failures related to the deadly 2025 UPS plane crash in Kentucky. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: Islamic Center shooters were radicalized online, "did not discriminate on who they hated," FBI says
item.description: Investigators gave an update Tuesday on Monday's deadly shooting at a mosque in San Diego. CBS News' Lana Zak and Anna Schecter have the latest.

item.title: Chinese execs, shipping container companies accused of price fixing during pandemic
item.description: The indictment, which was first reported by CBS News, marks the latest in a series of probes by the Justice Dept. related to the COVID-19 pandemic.

item.title: Is the teen summer job a thing of the past?
item.description: A summer job was once a seasonal tradition for millions of American teenagers. No more — here's why fewer young people are expected to clock in when school ends.

item.title: Tense exchange between Rep. Crow and CENTCOM commander over Iran, rules on quarter for enemies
item.description: Democratic Rep. Jason Crow of Colorado had a heated line of questioning for U.S. CENTCOM commander Adm. Brad Cooper about the rules regarding quarter during a House Armed Services Committee hearing on Tuesday. On March 13, Defense Sec. Pete Hegseth said, "We will keep pushing, keep advancing, no quarter, no mercy for our enemies," while speaking about the war with Iran.

item.title: Trump's $1.7+ billion fund sparks rush to capitalize: "All J6ers will apply"
item.description: One Republican lawyer close to the administration told CBS News that they expect the fund to face court challenges even though "a lot of people in MAGA world are already counting their money."

item.title: Polymarket to let users make prediction market bets on private companies
item.description: Polymarket users can now trade on private companies' valuations, IPOs and secondary market activity.

item.title: The 10 most affordable U.S. cities to buy a home
item.description: Buyers can still find lower-cost homes in some midsize cities, especially across the Rust Belt and Sun Belt, a new analysis finds.

item.title: Son reportedly a suspect in death of Mango fashion chain boss during hike
item.description: The only person with Mango clothing chain founder Isak Andic when he died on a hike was his eldest son, and his testimony was reportedly "inconsistent."

item.title: Why has the U.K. had 6 prime ministers in less than 10 years?
item.description: Political chaos has engulfed Britain, with six people holding the U.K.'s top job in a decade, including one prime minister who lasted only 45 days.

item.title: Iran more than doubled executions in 2025, global death penalty hit 44-year high
item.description: Iran executed more than twice as many people last year as it did in 2024, according to a new report by Amnesty International.

item.title: Austin shooting spree: 3 held after at least 12 incidents, police say
item.description: Mayor Kirk Watson said no motive has been identified and the shootings appear to be random.
